reviews.review_description: This is the most fun I have had playing a crafting survival game. It is the right mix of everything. Just hard enough, you don’t stay in any one area too long, you don’t have to spend all your time gathering resources, there are incentives to explore, and so much more. The dev team really did a great job making something that is fun to play.
This is pretty fun, but unfortunately it is single player. Still hoping we get a coop update/dlc/game. Probably one of the better VR survival games for me.

I really can't be bothered with this walking simulator of a tutorial while getting killed while using the portal, making me restart long parts of it.

The mechanics of the game are fine, it's just that the tutorial doesn't need to be a walking simulator.
I THINK THAT ONE THING THAT WOULD MAKE THIS GAME EVEN BETTER IS IF WE COULD TAME SOME OF THE ANIMALS (PREFERABLY A  VELOCIRAPTOR)

I really do like this survival game.  I feel like i need to Learn some more though.  Because i spend most of my time building a fire or finding wood.  I know the sleep and hunger things happen way too often at the start.  So  I cant really get anything else going at all by the time i must sleep or eat again,  which  sleeping raises the hunger.. along with cutting whichever animal you k¡lled. So u are right back to sleeping and eating. Nonstop back to back.  Wish it was spaced out more. 
If i can get that down to where Im not spending every moment doing that and i can actually explore, i will love it even more. 
Also where are the Gourds to store the tonic? 

There needs to be a section in the settings that lists how to craft/build each item. Since it only tells you once how to do each thing in the very beginning.. That would be nice.  I forgot how to make the club and it took me awhile to figure it out again. 
This game is addicting though.  And Fun. 
It really comes down to time management. What is priority etc.  
So once I get it all down,  I'm sure this will be another favorite game. 

Good Job!!!

I have been an avid oculus gamer since oculus 1 and have maybe 40% of games in the store. Barring daily wordle, connections and puzzling places, this is by far the best game i have, finished it more than 25x, over the years and still come back to it from time to time just to chill, hunt, get goosebumps with the great sound design.

This game plays very much like if Far Cry Primal were combined with a really hardcore survival game like Green Hell. I'm an IRL primitive survival expert (over 2,000 hours of IRL primitive survival camping) and this game is probably the closest thing you're going to find to the real deal. You craft things by doing abridged versions of the REAL steps to make them IRL, for example building a fire you break up sticks into smaller pieces, use a flint knife to process bark into wood fibers for a brush bundle, strike a spark into it to start the fire, add the small sticks, then larger sticks and eventually logs. If you want to make a spear that's just a sharp stick, you can use a flint knife to carve it by making the actual carving motions. Or you can make a stone spear point and lash it to a stick for an upgraded spear.

There's a bit of a supernatural element, animal spirit gods and the like, but that's what people believed in the stone age. 

On harder difficulty settings, night becomes deadly and you need to huddle by the fire and keep it going. Because danger and death are waiting for you in the dark. 

Hunting and processing the carcass for materials is a really nice touch, and if you're not careful you'll find that the day has gotten away from you and you've got very little time to shelter for the night, but the meat and materials from that kill are far too valuable to your survival to leave for scavengers, so you have to decide what you absolutely cannot afford to leave behind and finish processing those parts and HOPEFULLY leave yourself enough time to get back to your shelter and get a fire going to survive the night. 

I wish I could give this game 10 stars instead of 5. It's the most fun I've had in a VR game in a very long time. The balance of realism and still needing to be a fun game is PERFECT.

This game is brilliant. It's ignited a love for survival VR games for me. The levels are challenging but not impossible, the Wendigo is a pain in bum but once I learnt how to keep a fire going all night it wasn't an issue any more ( you add small/medium/large sticks as the circle around the fire says S/M/L) took me too long to figure it out and I kept dying lol. 
Thoroughly enjoyable and I was disappointed when it was finished. Would love a second one to be made

reviews.review_title: My favorite VR game so far!
reviews.review_description: SITS has become my go to game since I downloaded it several month ago - I've actually replayed this game by now, at least six or seven times (I've lost count actually) but each time I've replayed it, it's different or has had variable outcomes and unexpected surprises! I've got my favorite levels and least favorite levels (only because I hate lions and purple panthers lol!) they are the hardest to deal with I think. The UI is so well honed and fluid - it's one of the highlights IMO of this game over others I've played that are similar to this (Survival games) that it really seems so natural now. Oh, one other thing - this game is also beautiful visually - from an artistic standpoint incredible! If you like games such as Green Hell or Medieval Dynasty then you'll love this. I also would love to see a sequel of some kind to this game - maybe a prequel actually, sort of a vision of how he/or she became the great hunter in the past... The Hunter's Quest!  (there's a title for you!) lol!

I got this game with my 3 month quest+ trial and I can’t stop playing. I love everything about the game like the hunting, crafting and foraging and I plan to buy the game after my quest+ ends. This is the first vr survival game I’ve played and it has definitely left an extremely good impression on me.

I get what I need to do, but the lighting is so bad I keep getting lost and end up walking around in circles. Even using the map I have absolutely no idea what path or even what direction to go in. It's a shame because the mechanics seem fun to play with but I'm not going to waste my time on a game I keep getting lost in.
I spent the first 30 minutes of the game getting frustrated. I didn't even make it out of the tutorial area. Not worth it.

The game is really good from design to graphics. And I have ideas like a song in the smoke 2! You might be wondering how is that possible? You die at the end. But instead off the guy you originally play you play the eldest kid. so 30 years later your healer goes missing. then as the hunter you go to find her. Then I front of your own eyes you see your friend’s get quickly thrown away by something at lighting speed. You step back in terror as you see your friend’s die  and  little brother get impaled on a tree limb. You run back to the camp. Six months later your chief vowing vengeance on your brother but then boom. That’s as far as I’m going to say curse I can’t write this all day but more dinosaurs like “ T. rex alasoures gigantosouras brachiosaurus anklosuares” and so on and make bite markers on your hand scratch marks anything to tell the player I’m bleeding. And make is so that you can tame anamial like baby aknoysuares raptor. but the have health  and if the die they just spawn at camp. Don’t worry they’ll be fine. But in boss fights they won’t be there. And thell follow you into maps. Other than that make maps bigger more jungle like hard to see predators type jungle. But more maps like dessert ice etc. also make dinosaurs more aggressive towards each other. We all like a good Dino fight. Also food is something we want eggs are a big thing we want good food and baby dinosaurs. And different meat like raw steak type and that thing on the bone? And camp fire thing over the fire allot easier to cook. Also make it so you can burn bushes. And spears carve xl sticks and something will show up a spear and an arrow. ( Poison work the same on spears) And aquatic dinos not mosasaurs but bigger fish you can kill and they can attack you. Not all of them of course but like barracuda. And riding the pets not like baby's but like fully grown. And barryonx it's a apex predator make it 50/50 chance you can take and ride it. Other than song in the smoke 2... Good game keep creating
It's got cool artwork and all but the in game graphics and environment looks like it was done in crayon by an elementary school student. It's like PS2 graphics.

reviews.review_title: My favorite VE Game so far
reviews.review_description: Immersive and fun. Difficult and entertaining.

I've had the game for awhile just never got around to playing it. Until recent days. Yesterday I spent well over 4 hours in the game. I'm at the part where I have to kill the lion. Last night I dreamt I was getting chased by the raptors or whatever they are supposed to be. One things for certain I wouldn't want to run into one of those in real life. One thing though. What's the puffballs for ?
Whilst I liked the game it was just too complicated. I got fed fairly quickly.  Maybe I'll give the "special needs difficulty" a try.
Have never played a game with a concept like it. The crafting is rewarding and you are always discovering new things to bash together. I enjoyed the gameplay. All around great game.

I loved this game. It had me scrambling to find sticks for a fire place, food so I wouldn’t starve, and hunting to harvest bones and meat from animals. I enjoyed searching for a cave to setup camp and build my home.

Today is December 29th, 2024. I started this game on December 2nd and played almost every day—some days for four hours, some days for one hour, and most days for two hours. I've accomplished many things that ultimately didn't matter. I ended up with four creature skins and an extra bag. I've collected all the amulets for extra lives in every level except the last two, where I didn't obtain any and didn't set up a camp, just the fire.

I often wondered why I kept playing, especially since every level had the same objectives, even though it became increasingly difficult. However, I was absolutely addicted—I haven't played any game this frequently since "I Expect You to Die." The game consumed my thoughts, and I found myself planning my next moves throughout the day. I guess I really loved it, even though I’m not typically into survival games.
 
II tried another survival game, the green one, and I was really disappointed. I’m sure it works for some people, but I found it way too difficult. Maybe the developers aimed for a more realistic experience, but it felt unrealistic to me. Song in the Smoke, however, was perfect for me on normal mode.

reviews.review_title: I don’t know it’s good game though
reviews.review_description: The tutorial was basically the whole game for me there wasn’t much to do after words but it’s a fun game

reviews.review_title: Cool game but makes me sick
reviews.review_description: The game is cool, but I get nauseous playing it
Game is very old if you want a survival game like this play green hell it has multiplayer and it has better mechanics
I hope someday we get a sequel to this masterpiece.
Really bad walking mechanic in the game it’s so terrible that I played five minutes and put the game away and never touched it again. Don’t buy it.
I absolutely love this game but i wish it was multiplayer, i would LOVE to play this with my friends. Obviously include a singleplayer mode too but a multiplayer mode would be loads of fun
Have only just got the game but so far it is good I love the crafting system

I just finished this game, and I feel completely satisfied. It was tough at first, and I almost gave up, but I am so glad I stuck with it to to end. To those of you who like survival games, this is the game to play! Thank you to the makers. You did a great job!

DEVS PLS READ THIS
Very good survival game. The graphics are great on quest 3 standalone. The survival side is really present, you have to think about everything but above all focus on the essentials. The only reason I don't give 5 🌟 is because I think we should be able to cut wood ourselves and not just find it on the ground And there should be more varieties of danger and animals such as snakes or bats or mosquitoes, alligators or eagles... 
To compare with Green hell vr: I downloaded it I played 10min you always had to call Mia Mia Mia and follow a marked route so I uninstalled. Song in the smoke is better for me because you are alone in the jungle and you have to fend for yourself and not have to call Mia every 2min and hear her speak.... So buy this game with your eyes closed if you like survival games. DEVS PLEASE UPDATE WITH MORE ANIMALS AND DANGERS

Apparently the "Rekindled" version for PSVR2 is pretty nice but these devs couldn't be bothered to port the changes to Quest 3. If you go to their discord you hear whining and crying about how they didn't sell well enough. You know what makes for great PR and gives you a reason to advertise your game again? UPDATING THE MF.....

reviews.review_title: Not my Game but it could be...
reviews.review_description:
As good as Song in the Smoke proves to be, unfortunately it doesn't get me at all. 
Don't get me wrong the graphic style is pretty nice but the World just seems empty to me and if you compare it directly to Green Hell it's like night and day. 

In Song in the Smoke there are no holsters for weapons or tools, there is no backpack, you don't have to drink to survive, the VR crafting mechanics are very simple compared to Green Hell but what bothers me most about the game is that you Instead of climbing or jumping, you only have the option of teleporting. 
This constant teleporting around the map absolutely destroys the immersion for me. 
It is absolutely incomprehensible and disappointing that the developers completely ignore and abandoned the Quest version and do not update all the features of the "Rekindled" version of PSVR 2 for the Quest 3, because there is the option to jump manually and much more good stuff. 

If an update comes out at some point, which I don't think so, I will definitely take another look at the game.

Where to I begin with this absolute masterpiece to the VR community?
This game combines great but short storytelling, and a realistic survival experience to make one of the best games I’ve ever had the chance to try. Not only is his game cheap, enjoyable, and long, it’s also just the game I needed to survive 2023. Sure the graphics and animal will may be a bit buggy, but you won’t notice that in a visual landscape that will constantly have you exploring new things! And it’s really simplistic in its crafting, it’s combat, and it’s art style. And while you play this masterpiece, a story will unfold that will have you questioning its meaning, and even if you may survive to see the end of the story! 
I will spoil a bit here, so if you would like to skip this please do. The story goes something like this.
You are sick, or in a coma of some sorts, and you find yourself in a guided dream where what I would assume is your grandma guides you through this landscape to get to the end. You fight your way through, killing multiple lords like lions, deer, and more which I would assume mean different things in life so you can continue on.  And from quotes from the game, 
“Death travels on the wind.” “Death strikes from nowhere.” “Death cannot be stopped.”
I recommend you try the game out, and see if you can see this ending for yourself. 
I would sell my soul to get a sequel.
Edit- It is now officially 2025 and I am still confident on the fact this is the vr best game I have ever played. To the developers, I would give anything in my possession for the next game. I could build the story, I could major in coding to help make the game, I would do anything in my power for a sequel.

My first problem is no jumping or climbing just teleporting up, 2nd the tutorial gave me instructions for PS or Xbox, 3rd if I needed to switch hands with a torch the torch turns off and I need to light it up again, 4th the inventory is really clunky, i was expecting a backpack thing where you grab it from your back, lastly this game seems like you took a console game and poorly turned it into a VR game, if you want a game like this you should buy green hell which is same price as this but much better with better story and green hell is MEANT to be for VR, will be refunding.

reviews.review_title: FEW HOURS IN, I DIDN'T WANT TO COME OUT.
reviews.review_description:
Overall...this game is a beauty thus far. It's really immersive and gives me Lies Beneath type vibes only caveman survival instead of slaying horror. It's like walking around in a cell shaded oil painting that has you doing some things that are really intuitive and how you'd behave in your life when faced with the same situations. For example, putting an arrow through a big fish in a shallow pond and jumping in to get It and then spearing It on the end of a sharp stick and bringing It back to your campfire too cook It up while you relax and watch the stars and moon before feasting on It. I know It's gonna be a keeper and I'm gonna get my moneys worth and more out of It. I played my first run through until the battery died and not many games pull me in like that. The sound effects when playing with headphones at high volume are very realistic. I actually feel like I'm a caveman hunter and when I have to finally take off the headset I'm a bit bummed out cause there is more freedom for me in that world than this one I been enduring. This life beats you down worse than a lion coming right for you...

So far I only have two gripes. Had a bluetooth not tesponding crash in the tutorial eating some berries but I think It was caused by me putting the controller too close to my mouth/headset and It messed with the communication between them, (least I hope so and that doesn't happen again.)

My other concern is the brutal difficulty. I put two arrows with feathers directly through a deers head and brain and that sucker just ran away bleeding but never died after tracking him awhile, (I think It was actually laughing at me when It went back to the river to drink some water like nothing happened.)

This game is fantastic.  The environments are beautiful, and fun to explore.  Learning how to craft weapons and utilize them effectively was immensely gratifying. The sound effects and music are perfect. This is the best survival game I have played and am amazed that the developers were able to create this for the Quest. 

Thank you developers for creating this jewel. I among others truly appreciate your hard work.

I’ve tried to contact 17bit games regarding the issue but so far I haven’t got an answer. 
Currently playing on the 4th level on Oculus Quest 2 version and although I’ve completed the sequences in descriptions of the achievements they are locked for me.

I would update the review once the issue will be resolved.

developer_response.body: Hello - the Achievements issue has been resolved!  Please give it a whirl!

reviews.review_title: Hits all the bases except one
reviews.review_description: This is  truly one of  the    most immersive, fun, intense games I've played on VR.  Crafting, gameplay, audio, visually  it's  stellar.   My hope would be that anyone who has an interest could play this title. However, they can't because there are no difficulty settings. Many people because their age, skill level experience  with gaming etc  could  find this title frustrating  and give up.  Which is a major  shame because the  game is so bloody good! Why not have  easy, medium and hard  difficulty settings ? I don't  get it. It would increase  access and  no doubt increase replayability. That said, this  game is amazing!
All good

This game is incredibly immersive and it really allows you too be a prehistoric person and experience the fears and struggles. I would totally recommend to anyone who wants a survival game. It is amazing

reviews.review_title: A VR survival game at its best
reviews.review_description:
I've played a lot of saints and sinners and dig it , but was looking for that real survival like the long dark ( great console survival)  so I follow this guy . Shout out to VRmachine on you tube and seen he had this game  song in the smoke . So I started watch  .. 2 episodes in I was sold ! How did I not find this game sooner ..   the story is you just keep moving through the land in search of a song you hear and visions   But the true story is the survival , I'm on the 4 th or 5th land   Just slow playing getting the best I can get and hording everything.  Idk what will happen if I finish , if it ends I'll start over on  harder difficulty.. my hope is for a never ending survival mode !!!   
Great job team  over at SITS!
Whish you could climb without teleporting, but other than that an amazing game
